learning_outcomes.description: apply knowledge of specific mental illnesses and mental health service delivery to demonstrate a client or woman centered approach when engaging with people experiencing mental health issues.
apply the principles of recovery and evidence-based approaches to mental health promotion, early intervention, and illness management across the lifespan, including in the perinatal period.
explain the role of the multidisciplinary team and the values, ethical standards, professional and legal responsibilities, and theoretical frameworks that underpin mental health service.

demonstrate the capacity to plan and deliver recovery-oriented care in a simulated environment that addresses the needs of people with mental health concerns, across the lifespan and in a variety of settings.
describe the differing practice issues in mental health services across diverse cultural population groups, including Aboriginal and or Torres Strait Islander peoples.

assessments.task_description:
An Inspera-based quiz covering course content from Weeks 1-3 will be made available from 10am on 17 August until 6:00pm on 21 August.

  • The quiz will consist of 15 multiple-choice questions.
  • The quiz is open-book.
  • Students will have 30 minutes to complete the quiz once it has commenced.


The Teaching Team are aware that you are under a great deal of pressure regarding work and study demands, and that financially things are very challenging. We don't want the demands of study to increase your stress - but at the same time we can't simply remove all demands from the course. Therefore, we have decided the following:

This task is optional. It is worth 10% of your final mark, but you do not have to complete it if you do not wish to. The only non-optional task in this course is the final group task.

Because the task is optional, it will not be made available beyond the already generous opening and closing times/dates.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The Teaching Team are aware that you are under a great deal of pressure regarding work and study demands, and that financially things are very challenging. We don't want the demands of study to increase your stress - but at the same time we can't simply remove all demands from the course. Therefore, we have decided the following:
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: This quiz is available for five days. No extensions beyond that window will be granted.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This task involves groups of students submitting a recorded case analysis.

For full details, please refer to the course Blackboard site.

This is a compulsory task. You will must submit the required work in order to be eligible to pass the course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
